You are implementing a design from a design file (`ReviewLens AI.dc.html`) into an existing Rails 8.1 app at `/Users/dpaluy/projects/ReviewLens.ui`. The design is already fully read and understood. You need to implement it faithfully. ## What to implement The design has two routes: - **Route A** (`/products/new`): Review ingestion form with URL scrape / CSV upload tabs - **Route B** (`/products/:id`): Product show page with 4 collapsible sections ### Design tokens - Font family: Inter (body) + JetBrains Mono (code/mono) - Background: `#f7f7f5` - Primary green: `#1f8a5b` (dark: `#1a754d`, light bg: `#eaf5ef`, light border: `#d4e8dd`, text: `#166b47`) - Dark text: `#16191d` - Muted text: `#565b62` - Light text: `#8a9099` - Border: `#e6e6e2` - Card bg: `#fff` - Error: `#b4382f` (bg `#fbece9`, border `#f0cdc7`) - Warning amber: `#c08a1e` (bg `#fbf3e2`, border `#efdcae`) CSS keyframes needed: ```css @keyframes rl-pulse { 0%,100%{box-shadow:0 0 0 4px rgba(31,138,91,0.18);}50%{box-shadow:0 0 0 8px rgba(31,138,91,0.04);} } @keyframes rl-shimmer { 0%{background-position:200% 0;}100%{background-position:-200% 0;} } @keyframes rl-blink { 0%,80%,100%{opacity:0.2;}40%{opacity:1;} } ``` ## Files to create/modify ### 1. `app/assets/stylesheets/application.css` Add after `@import "tailwindcss";`: ```css *,*::before,*::after{box-sizing:border-box;} body{margin:0;font-family:'Inter',sans-serif;background:#f7f7f5;min-height:100vh;color:#16191d;-webkit-font-smoothing:antialiased;} ::placeholder{color:#aeb4bb;} input,button,select,textarea{font-family:inherit;} @keyframes rl-pulse{0%,100%{box-shadow:0 0 0 4px rgba(31,138,91,0.18);}50%{box-shadow:0 0 0 8px rgba(31,138,91,0.04);}} @keyframes rl-shimmer{0%{background-position:200% 0;}100%{background-position:-200% 0;}} @keyframes rl-blink{0%,80%,100%{opacity:0.2;}40%{opacity:1;}} ``` ### 2. `app/views/layouts/application.html.erb` Replace existing content with: ```erb <%= content_for(:title) || "ReviewLens" %> <%= csrf_meta_tags %> <%= csp_meta_tag %> <%= yield :head %> <%= stylesheet_link_tag :app, "data-turbo-track": "reload" %> <%= javascript_importmap_tags %>
<%= link_to root_path, style: "display:flex;align-items:center;gap:10px;text-decoration:none;color:inherit;" do %>
ReviewLens ANALYST <% end %>
<%= yield :header_action %>
<%= yield %> ``` ### 3. `app/views/products/new.html.erb` Full redesign matching Route A. Uses a Stimulus `import-mode` controller for the URL/CSV tab toggle. The "URL scrape" tab uses `import_mode: "url"` and shows the URL input. The "CSV upload" tab uses `import_mode: "manual"` and shows a drag-drop zone. The sample URL button fills the input. The form uses `form_with model: @product`. Key aspects: - Segmented control tabs (URL scrape / CSV upload) - URL input in JetBrains Mono - "Try:" chip that fills sample URL: `trustpilot.com/review/quickbooks.intuit.com` - CSV section: download sample link + dashed drop zone + column list - Error messages when present - Green "Ingest Reviews" submit button The form must pass `product[import_mode]` (hidden field set by JS), `product[source_url]`, `product[manual_reviews]` (for CSV mode, hidden textarea), `product[name]` (hidden, optional). ### 4. `app/views/products/show.html.erb` Full redesign matching Route B. State variables (compute at top of view): ```ruby pipeline = @product.ingestion_status # string sec_failed = @product.failed? || @ingestion_run.failed? sec_ready = @product.ready? sec_full = sec_ready && @product.reviews_count > 0 sec_empty = sec_ready && @product.reviews_count == 0 sec_skeleton = !sec_ready && !sec_failed is_thin = sec_full && @product.thin_corpus? skipped = @ingestion_run.reviews_skipped.to_i show_warnings = sec_full && skipped > 0 ``` Product header row: - Breadcrumb `/products/{external_id}` in JetBrains Mono - H1: product name (or external_id) - Row of badges: platform badge (green dot + "Trustpilot"), source_url badge, review count badge (only when ready) Section 01 (Ingestion status) - collapsible via Stimulus `collapsible` controller: - Header row: number "01", title "Ingestion status", collapsed summary badge (failed/ready), chevron - Content (when open): - Stepper with 5 steps: Queued, Fetching, Parsing, Summarizing, Ready - Green progress line behind stepper - 3 counter cells: Pages attempted, Reviews found, Reviews imported - Parser warnings banner (when `show_warnings`) - Failed banner with "Try manual import" button (when `sec_failed`) Stepper step states: - `done`: green circle (bg:#1f8a5b text:#fff border:#1f8a5b) with "✓" symbol, bold dark label - `active`: white circle with green border + rl-pulse animation, green label - `failed`: red circle (bg:#b4382f) with "✕" symbol, red label - `todo`: white circle with #e6e6e2 border, light gray label Progress line width logic: failed→12%, else `(cur_idx/4*100)%` where cur_idx = index in `[pending,fetching,parsing,summarizing,ready]`. Section 02 (Corpus summary) - collapsible: - When `sec_full`: - Thin corpus warning (if `is_thin`) - 4-card stats grid: Reviews imported, Average rating (#{avg}★ /5), Sentiment split (progress bar + percentages), Date range - Rating distribution bars (5★ through 1★) - Field coverage table - When `sec_skeleton`: shimmer skeletons - When `sec_empty`: "0 reviews imported" + "Try manual import" button - When `sec_failed`: "Summary unavailable" text Section 03 (Sample reviews) - collapsible: - When `sec_full`: - Table with columns: Rating, Title, Excerpt, Source - Each row: rating badge (color-coded), title (truncated), body excerpt (truncated), source URL chip - Rating badge colors: >=4 green, ==3 gray, <3 red - When `sec_skeleton`: shimmer rows - When `sec_empty`/`sec_failed`: locked messages Section 04 (Ask the reviews): - NOT collapsible - header always visible with lock badges for non-full states - When `sec_full`: - Suggested question chips (5 chips): "Top pain points", "What users praise", "Objections that hurt sales", "Repeated feature requests", "5 negative quotes" - ``: - If no messages: empty state card - Messages list (user messages + AI answer cards + refusal cards) - "Thinking" indicator (hidden by default) - Input row: text input + "Ask" button (form POST to `product_chat_messages_path(@product)`) - Blocked question chips: "How do G2 reviews compare?", "Current weather?", "Better than Zapier?" - When skeleton/failed/empty: lock messages For the collapsible sections, start them all closed. Section 01 opens automatically if the product failed (add `data-collapsible-open-value="true"` when sec_failed). Auto-poll while ingesting: Add `` to the `:head` yield when sec_skeleton. ### 5. `app/javascript/controllers/import_mode_controller.js` ```js import { Controller } from "@hotwired/stimulus" export default class extends Controller { static targets = ["urlSection", "csvSection", "urlBtn", "csvBtn", "modeInput"] connect() { this.showUrl() } showUrl() { this.urlSectionTarget.hidden = false this.csvSectionTarget.hidden = true this.modeInputTarget.value = "url" this._activate(this.urlBtnTarget, this.csvBtnTarget) } showCsv() { this.urlSectionTarget.hidden = true this.csvSectionTarget.hidden = false this.modeInputTarget.value = "manual" this._activate(this.csvBtnTarget, this.urlBtnTarget) } _activate(on, off) { const onStyle = "flex:1;padding:7px 10px;border:none;border-radius:7px;font-size:13px;font-weight:500;cursor:pointer;background:#fff;color:#16191d;box-shadow:0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.10);" const offStyle = "flex:1;padding:7px 10px;border:none;border-radius:7px;font-size:13px;font-weight:500;cursor:pointer;background:transparent;color:#6b7280;" on.style.cssText = onStyle off.style.cssText = offStyle } } ``` ### 6. `app/javascript/controllers/collapsible_controller.js` ```js import { Controller } from "@hotwired/stimulus" export default class extends Controller { static targets = ["content", "chevron"] static values = { open: Boolean } connect() { if (this.hasContentTarget) { this.contentTarget.hidden = !this.openValue } this._updateChevron() } toggle() { this.openValue = !this.openValue if (this.hasContentTarget) { this.contentTarget.hidden = !this.openValue } this._updateChevron() } _updateChevron() { if (this.hasChevronTarget) { this.chevronTarget.style.transform = this.openValue ? "rotate(180deg)" : "" } } } ``` ### 7. `app/javascript/controllers/chat_controller.js` Handles chip fills and blocked question submissions: ```js import { Controller } from "@hotwired/stimulus" export default class extends Controller { static targets = ["input"] fill(event) { this.inputTarget.value = event.params.question this.inputTarget.focus() } fireBlocked(event) { this.inputTarget.value = event.params.question this.element.querySelector("form").requestSubmit() } onKeydown(event) { if (event.key === "Enter" && !event.shiftKey) { event.preventDefault() event.target.closest("form").requestSubmit() } } } ``` ### 8. `config/routes.rb` Add nested chat_messages resource: ```ruby Rails.application.routes.draw do root "products#new" resources :products, only: %i[new create show] do resources :chat_messages, only: [:create] end get "up" => "rails/health#show", as: :rails_health_check end ``` ### 9. `app/controllers/chat_messages_controller.rb` ```ruby class ChatMessagesController < ApplicationController before_action :find_product def create question = params[:question].to_s.strip return redirect_to @product if question.blank? session[:chat] ||= {} session[:chat][@product.id.to_s] ||= [] session[:chat][@product.id.to_s] << { "role" => "user", "text" => question, "at" => Time.current.iso8601 } # AI processing placeholder – wire up ReviewAnalysis::QuestionAnswerer when ready answer = { "role" => "assistant", "answer" => "AI analysis is being configured. Check back soon.", "confidence" => "low", "ids" => [], "limitations" => "AI services not yet configured." } session[:chat][@product.id.to_s] << answer redirect_to @product end private def find_product @product = Product.find(params[:product_id]) redirect_to root_path unless @product.ready? && @product.reviews_count > 0 end end ``` ### 10. `app/controllers/products_controller.rb` Update the `show` action to load sample reviews, compute stats, and load chat messages: ```ruby def show @product = Product.find(params[:id]) @ingestion_run = @product.ingestion_runs.order(:created_at).last raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, "Ingestion run missing" unless @ingestion_run if @product.ready? && @product.reviews_count > 0 @sample_reviews = @product.reviews.order(rating: :desc, created_at: :asc).limit(10) @field_coverage = compute_field_coverage end @chat_messages = (session.dig(:chat, @product.id.to_s) || []) end private def compute_field_coverage total = @product.reviews_count.to_f return [] if total.zero? reviews = @product.reviews rows = [ ["Star rating", reviews.where.not(rating: nil).count], ["Review title", reviews.where.not(title: [nil, ""]).count], ["Body text", reviews.where.not(body: [nil, ""]).count], ["Author name", reviews.where.not(reviewer_label: [nil, ""]).count], ["Review date", reviews.where.not(review_date: nil).count], ["Reviewer role", reviews.where.not(reviewer_role: [nil, ""]).count], ["Company size", reviews.where.not(reviewer_company_size: [nil, ""]).count], ] rows.map do |field, count| pct = (count / total * 100).round bar_color = pct >= 80 ? "#1f8a5b" : pct >= 40 ? "#c08a1e" : "#c2c6cc" ink_color = pct >= 80 ? "#166b47" : pct >= 40 ? "#8a5e10" : "#8a9099" { field:, pct:, bar_color:, ink_color: } end end ``` ## Style fidelity notes The inline styles should match the design file exactly. Use the exact hex colors, border-radius values, font sizes, and spacing from the design. The show page must render correctly for all four states: `sec_full`, `sec_empty`, `sec_failed`, `sec_skeleton`. ## Things to be careful about 1. The `compute_field_coverage` helper method must be added to the `private` section of `ProductsController`, keeping `create_url_import`, `create_manual_import`, `manual_import?`, `product_params`, and `manual_product_params` also private. 2. The collapsible sections use data attributes: `data-controller="collapsible"`, `data-action="click->collapsible#toggle"`, `data-collapsible-content-target`, `data-collapsible-chevron-target`, and optionally `data-collapsible-open-value="true"` for initially-open sections. 3. The import mode controller uses: `data-controller="import-mode"`, `data-import-mode-target="urlSection"`, `data-import-mode-target="csvSection"`, `data-import-mode-target="urlBtn"`, `data-import-mode-target="csvBtn"`, `data-import-mode-target="modeInput"`, `data-action="click->import-mode#showUrl"`, `data-action="click->import-mode#showCsv"`. 4. The chat controller uses: `data-controller="chat"`, `data-chat-target="input"`, `data-action="click->chat#fill"`, `data-chat-question-param="..."`, `data-action="click->chat#fireBlocked"`, `data-action="keydown->chat#onKeydown"`. 5. The rating distribution for section 02 comes from `@product.rating_distribution` (jsonb, keys are strings like "5", "4"...). The max value is used for bar widths. 6. Sentiment from `@product.sentiment_distribution` (jsonb with keys "positive", "neutral", "negative" as integers or floats representing percentages). 7. Date range from `@product.oldest_review_at` and `@product.newest_review_at` (datetime columns). 8. For the "Try manual import" buttons in failed/empty states, they link to `new_product_path(import_mode: "manual")` and the products#new action should use `params[:import_mode]` to pre-select the tab. 9. The new page must show errors when `@product.errors.any?`. 10. All new JS controllers must be placed in `app/javascript/controllers/` and will be auto-loaded by stimulus-loading. Please implement all 10 files. Write code, not analysis. Match the design as closely as possible while keeping the Rails patterns intact.

class ChatMessagesController < ApplicationController
  before_action :find_product

  def create
    question = params[:question].to_s.strip
    return redirect_to @product if question.blank?

    session[:chat] ||= {}
    session[:chat][@product.id.to_s] ||= []
    session[:chat][@product.id.to_s] << { "role" => "user", "text" => question, "at" => Time.current.iso8601 }

    # AI processing placeholder – wire up ReviewAnalysis::QuestionAnswerer when ready
    answer = {
      "role" => "assistant",
      "answer" => "AI analysis is being configured. Check back soon.",
      "confidence" => "low",
      "ids" => [],
      "limitations" => "AI services not yet configured."
    }
    session[:chat][@product.id.to_s] << answer

    redirect_to @product
  end

  private

  def find_product
    @product = Product.find(params[:product_id])
    redirect_to root_path unless @product.ready? && @product.reviews_count > 0
  end
end

    def compute_field_coverage
      total = @product.reviews_count.to_f
      return [] if total.zero?

      reviews = @product.reviews
      rows = [
        [ "Star rating",    reviews.where.not(rating: nil).count ],
        [ "Review title",   reviews.where.not(title: [ nil, "" ]).count ],

        [ "Author name",    reviews.where.not(reviewer_label: [ nil, "" ]).count ],
        [ "Review date",    reviews.where.not(review_date: nil).count ],
        [ "Reviewer role",  reviews.where.not(reviewer_role: [ nil, "" ]).count ],
        [ "Company size",   reviews.where.not(reviewer_company_size: [ nil, "" ]).count ],
      ]

      rows.map do |field, count|
        pct = (count / total * 100).round
        bar_color = pct >= 80 ? "#1f8a5b" : pct >= 40 ? "#c08a1e" : "#c2c6cc"
        ink_color  = pct >= 80 ? "#166b47" : pct >= 40 ? "#8a5e10" : "#8a9099"

8. `/config/routes.rb` - Added nested `chat_messages` resource under `products`.

9. `/app/controllers/chat_messages_controller.rb` - New controller storing messages in session, with placeholder AI response.

10. `/app/controllers/products_controller.rb` - Updated `show` action to load `@sample_reviews`, `@field_coverage`, and `@chat_messages`; added private `compute_field_coverage` helper.

**Tests updated** (`test/integration/ingestion_status_test.rb`, `products_flow_test.rb`, `manual_import_flow_test.rb`) to match the new HTML structure (no more `dt`/`dd`/`h2` assertions; updated to check `data-testid` + `p` tag text, hidden input instead of radio). All 64 tests pass.
